$comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the AI's brain. Both the item finder/rater (scout/) and the control center read this. When Mehmet feeds new info and Claude distills it, this file is updated -> the scout and the dashboard both update. See ai-system/ingestion-pipeline.md.

exceptions.source: Non-returnable grocery can clear the ROI gate at 25% instead of 30% — profit is locked once sold, no return risk. (field-sops.md / sourcing-playbook.md)

criteria.source: SINGLE SOURCE — scout/config.py loads these criteria from here; playbooks/sourcing-playbook.md explains them

pennyWar.status: proposed default — tune after live Keepa history is available (Phase 2, Prompt 2.1)

guards.source: SINGLE SOURCE — scout/config.py loads these learned red-flag thresholds from here. priceSpikeRatio: reject/penalize when current price > 1.5x its 90-day avg (likely to revert). offersRiseRatio: flag when new-offer count > 1.4x its 90-day avg (seller spike -> price tank). amazonBuyBoxShareMax: hard-reject when Amazon wins the Buy Box >= 20% of the time (rotation), even if a 3P holds it now. pennyWar: proposed threshold for a Buy-Box price-war detector — not yet wired to real data (needs Keepa history=True, Phase 2). restrictionKeywords: moved here from a hardcoded dict in scout/scoring.py (_RESTRICTION_KEYWORDS) so both the scout AND the control-center Find page read the identical heuristic word-boundary list — a hint, never an eligibility verdict; always verify FBA restrictions/hazmat/expiration/meltable status in Seller Central before buying. currentVsAvg90PriceCaution: Scout Agent Build Plan (2026-07-02) sec 4.1 — a SOFTER, non-gate caution adjustment (small point penalty) at 1.15x avg90, distinct from and evaluated separately from priceSpikeRatio's 1.5x HARD flag; gives an earlier warning without touching the existing gate.

grocery.source: Code Review 2026-07-02, Finding CS6: referralRates.grocery (flat 0.08) overstated profit/ROI for anything above $15 — the real Amazon rule (see referralRates.source above) is 8% at or below $15, 15% above it, and most of the $8-$60 OA price band sits ABOVE $15. Used by scout/config.referral_rate_for(category, price) and the control-center deal-analyzer when both a category and a price are known; referralRates.grocery (0.08) remains the fallback for grocery when no price is available to band on.

fees.source: Category referral rates simplified from Amazon's real fee table (knowledge-rag/sources/amazon/selling-fees.md, collected 2026-06-23 from sell.amazon.com/pricing). Several categories are tiered by price (clothing 5% <=$15 / 10% $15-20 / 17% >$20; grocery 8% <=$15 / 15% above; baby 8% <=$10 / 15% above; electronics accessories 15% <=$100 / 8% above) — the value stored here is the rate that applies across most of the $8-$60 OA price band (criteria.priceMin/priceMax). Consult the source doc before trusting an edge-case price point. 'default' (0.15) covers any category not listed; $0.30 minReferralFee applies to all categories per Amazon policy. fuelSurcharge (3.5%, effective 2026-04) applies to the FBA fulfillment fee only, per the 2026 fee-change research (research-inbox/research-insights.md). prepCost ($0.50/unit) reflects the seller now prepping/labeling FBA in the US as of 2026 (Amazon ended its own FBA prep). Moved from scout/config.py's + control-center's separately-hardcoded FUEL_RATE/PREP constants into this single source (Code Review 2026-07-02, Finding S6) — both now read this block, with matching code fallbacks if it's absent. automotive/industrial added at 12% (selling-fees.md: 'Automotive & Powersports' and 'Business, Industrial & Scientific' rows) and garden/arts_crafts/musical_instruments added explicitly at 15% (selling-fees.md: 'Lawn & Garden', 'Musical Instruments & AV'; arts_crafts has no dedicated row so uses the 15% 'Everything Else' rate) — Full-crew ML audit, 2026-07-11: fba-deal-calculator found the 5 categories added with the 18-category rotation expansion (2260cb0) were silently taking the 15% default, overstating the assumed referral cost 3pp for the 2 that are really 12%. inboundShipPerLb ($0.60/lb, range $0.50-0.80 per fba-selleramp-analyst's documented cost stack) added the same day — the profit/ROI estimate and the backtest's would_have_profited labels previously omitted inbound-shipping-to-FBA entirely (live-proven +$0.59/unit at 1lb to +$3.00/unit at 5lb optimistic drift); now subtracted in scoring.estimate_oa_profit_roi and scoring.net_proceeds via scoring.estimate_inbound_shipping.

preferredOffers.source: The 5-7 seller 'goldilocks' offer band is a scoring BONUS, not a gate — the hard offer band stays criteria.minOffers/maxOffers (3-25) and is unaffected. (learning-hub/transcripts/insights.md #9)

adjustments.source: Point deltas applied on top of the 0-100 base score (scout/scoring.py's OA_WEIGHTS) — none of these are gates; a candidate can trigger several and still clear the review threshold. Values are the scout's original hardcoded magnitudes, moved here so tuning is a brain edit, not a code edit (Code Review 2026-07-02, Finding S5). scout/config.py reads this block with code fallbacks matching these exact numbers. priceOutOfBand added 2026-07-13 (SOURCING_AND_QUEUE_PLAN.md Phase 1.4, Mehmet-approved 'I give full permission for everything'): criteria.priceMin/priceMax ($8-$60) was documented as a pass gate but scoring.py's OA path never checked it — live-reproduced leads at $80-$191 scored 72-91 with zero price signal. GRADUATED, not flat (deal-exam-calibrated, scout/config.py's OA_PRICE_BAND_PENALTY_SPAN_MULT=3): a flat -8 crushed real narrator-endorsed deals ($7/257%-ROI, $84, $96.50, all called 'healthy'/'good' in sourced transcripts) as hard as a genuinely bad $190 outlier — full magnitude only applies 3 band-widths ($156) beyond either boundary.

ipCliffShape.source: IP-complaint 'cliff' heuristic: a listing whose 90-day avg offer count was once >= minAvgOffers (a real crowd existed) and whose CURRENT offer count has collapsed to <= maxCurrentOffers (e.g. 56->1). Moved from scout/scoring.py's hardcoded 8/2 (Finding S5).

scoring.rankingChampionSource: Review fix (2026-07-06) — HUMAN-ONLY promotion switch for the cloud-trained ranker (scout/train_ranker.py). Every report/Discord post has always SAID promotion works this way, but until this fix nothing in the codebase ever actually read this key — training ran, evaluated, and uploaded a champion/challenger comparison every cadence with zero possible effect on anything. 'rule' (default, shadow-only): the deterministic triage formula (scoring.triage_score) orders the Review Queue/winners exactly as it always has; the trained challenger still trains and reports every cadence but has ZERO effect on what gets shown, gated, or bought. 'challenger': a human has read the challenger's win/lose verdict in learning-hub/tracking/ranker-report.md and explicitly promoted it here — the trained model's probability (train_ranker.challenger_score, via scout/pipeline.py's _evaluate/run_once) now orders the winners list, but it STILL NEVER touches the hard score>=threshold gate, hazmat/IP/eligibility checks, or any other compliance/safety rule — those stay rule-based always, and no purchase is ever automatic either way. Flip back to 'rule' any time to revert instantly, nothing destructive. Edit via fba-brain-updater only.
scoring.source: scoreThreshold/topN mirror scout/config.py's SCORE_THRESHOLD/TOP_N .env defaults (only send picks >= this score, max picks per run). assumedDailyTokens is used by propose_updates.py to flag real drift (compares a 14-run average against assumedDailyTokens*0.5/1.5 — purely a monitoring figure, nothing in collect_hourly.py/backtest.py is gated off it). Moved from scattered code constants (Code Review 2026-07-02, Finding S5) — scout/config.py falls back to these same values if this file is absent. marginHealthThreshold (added for Finding R3-nits) single-sources the control-center deal-analyzer's Margin-stat-tile health indicator (green above, neutral below) — it was independently hardcoded there alongside worstCaseLossBarUsd's own local literal copy; both now read this block instead of duplicating the numbers. (Restored 2026-07-11 — Full-crew ML audit found commit fee4f64 had silently dropped this line when it added rankingChampion/rankingChampionSource; recovered verbatim from git history, `git show fee4f64 -- learning-hub/data/ai-brain.json`.) 2026-07-11: assumedDailyTokens corrected 7500->1500 (Mehmet-approved data-driven proposal, brain-proposals.md 2026-07-11 02:00 UTC) — the old 7500 was SYSTEM_BLUEPRINT.md §3's budget against the unpurchased €49/mo Keepa Product Finder tier (20 tokens/min); the collector actually deployed (scout/collect_hourly.py, keepa_client.py) runs on the cheaper €19/mo Data-subscription trickle, which refills at 1 token/min ≈ 1,440/day — 1500 rounds that up. The old value made the drift alarm's low-band (7500*0.5=3750/day) mathematically unreachable under the real deployed plan, so it had permanently false-alarmed every day since 2026-07-08 regardless of actual scout behavior.

seasonality.source: Seasonal buy windows from learning-hub/playbooks/field-sops.md's seasonal calendar. buyLeadWeeks = how many weeks ahead of a brand's peak-demand window to buy so inventory lands in time for the seasonal BSR hump. 2026-07-11: removed LEGO from q4.brands (full-crew audit companion edit to the brands.friendly->avoid move below — this key isn't read by any code yet, but would otherwise tell a future seasonality detector to plan LEGO purchases around Q4 while brands.avoid permanently hard-rejects every LEGO candidate year-round; Mehmet-approved alongside the brands fix).

productFinderStack.source: The scout's default Keepa Product Finder recipe per friendly brand: BSR <= 200k (top ~1% of category), Amazon out of stock (leaves the Buy Box open to 3P sellers), >= 4 offers (confirms real OA competition, not a single-seller wholesale/PL listing). (learning-hub/playbooks/sourcing-playbook.md reverse-sourcing method; learning-hub/transcripts/insights.md #8)

brands.source: scout/brands.py (seeded from playbooks/brands-and-sources.md). 2026-07-11: LEGO + Under Armour moved friendly->avoid — Mehmet-approved compliance-driven proposal (brain-proposals.md 2026-07-11 08:03 UTC), evidence: Jul-1-2026 750+-brand gated tracker 'Fully gated' tier + IP-complaint-playbook 'zero-tolerance' tier (research-inbox/research-insights.md), Under Armour empirically brand-gated in 19/30 recent leads. This is a hard, unconditional gate (scoring.oa_hard_reject via brands.is_avoided), not just a scoring-bonus removal — both brands stop surfacing as picks entirely, though candidates are still collected pre-gate as training data (scout/pipeline.py persists before the hard-reject filter runs).

tokenBudget.source: SUPERSEDED as an enforced split by Session 54's hourly burst collector (scout/collect_hourly.py, keepa-collect.yml) - it no longer allocates a FIXED daily amount per tier; instead every dispatch reads the ACTUAL currently-banked tokensLeft and spends it waterfall-style, never waiting for a refill. The three legacy allocation numbers remain DEFAULT fallbacks for standalone/manual invocations. refillRatePerMinute is non-secret current-plan telemetry for honest dashboard utilization math, live-verified from Keepa's official /token response on 2026-07-10; it is NOT a spending limit and must be updated after any plan change. Live-confirmed costs: Product Finder API is REQUEST_REJECTED on this plan (search fallback 10 tokens/term); history pulls cost 1 token/ASIN.

learning.source: System Blueprint Prompt 3.1 — the label builder (scout/labels.py) refuses to assemble a training table below this many labeled rows (with both positive and negative outcomes represented). Below it, the scout runs on the transparent rule score alone; this is a floor for even ATTEMPTING calibration, not a promotion threshold — scout_pro's champion/challenger gate (PROMOTION_MIN_GAIN) is the separate, stricter bar for actually replacing the rule engine.
learning.harvesterSource: DATA_ENGINE_PLAN.md V0 #4 — scout/harvest.py idle-token harvester. harvesterEnabled is FALSE on the Keepa Pro trickle (1 token/min, 60 bank: no idle surplus to bank; harvest.enabled() returns a blocked-on-upgrade status). Flip to true only after upgrading to the 20/min API tier. harvestTokenShare (0..1) is the fraction of the OBSERVED daily token generation the harvester may spend once enabled (harvest.observed_daily_generation reads the real refill rate; never an assumed tier).
learning.backtestSource: DATA_ENGINE_PLAN.md V2 — scout/backtest.py hard per-run token ceiling (backtest.backtest_token_cap). The backtest samples ~3-5k on-policy ASINs (1 token each, history=365) and windows them into ~50k derived training rows; the cap keeps a run from draining the Pro-trickle bank, and the job is resumable across days toward the corpus target.

sampling.avoidBrands: collect as data, flag ip_risk, NEVER candidates
sampling.source: Session 55 (2026-07-05) — Mehmet's directive: DATA collection (the backtest/training corpus scout/backtest.py + scout/deals_firehose.py build) must be brand-AGNOSTIC and as broad as the token budget allows; brand lists (brands.py OA_FRIENDLY_BRANDS/AVOID_BRANDS) remain ONLY for buy-candidate discovery (pipeline.py, discovery_hints.py) and purchase gating (scoring.oa_hard_reject) — this block does not change either of those. brandFilter=NONE means the explore/dealfeed sampling paths apply NO friendly/avoid brand seeding at all (categories/price/BSR only); an avoid-listed brand's ASIN can still be COLLECTED as training data, tagged ip_risk=true on its backtest_rows row, but it can never become a lead/candidate/review-queue item (that hard gate is untouched, tested in scout/tests/test_backtest_sampling.py). sampleSourceTags label which mechanism produced an ASIN: onpolicy = the existing friendly+hint brand-seeded sample (scout/backtest.py sample_asins_on_policy, unchanged); explore = brand-agnostic category-keyword search via the same Product-Finder-rejected-on-Pro search fallback (scout/backtest.py sample_asins_explore); dealfeed = the Keepa /deal endpoint firehose (scout/deals_firehose.py, ~5 tokens per <=150-deal page — UNVERIFIED live cost/schema until the account's negative balance recovers and a real dispatch runs, see AI_COLLABORATION_JOURNAL.md Session 55). priceBands/bsrStrata are computed POST-HOC per training row (from each ASIN's own observed price/BSR at each simulation window, not a live Keepa pre-filter — Product Finder's range filters are unavailable on this Pro plan) and reported as a composition breakdown, not a hard pre-selection quota.
sampling.tier1ReserveFractionSource: Keepa throughput plan Action C (Cowork, 2026-07-11) — Mehmet-approved ('I approved all of the proposals, do them'). Temporarily lowers scout/collect_hourly.py's TIER1_RESERVE_FRACTION from its 0.25 code default to 0.15 for the collection sprint targeting 10k unique ASINs: shadow-outcome rechecks (tier 1) skim less of every hour's bank, leaving more for tier 3's history pulls that actually grow backtest_rows. Deliberately NOT zeroed — shadow validation is how the ranker eventually earns promotion evidence, so tier 1 still runs, just capped tighter. Reversible: delete this key (or raise it back toward 0.25) to revert; collect_hourly._tier1_reserve_fraction() falls back to the 0.25 code constant if this key is ever absent/invalid. Revisit once new-ASINs/day sustains the plan's ~400+/day target for a few days.

corpusAcceleration.source: APPROVED by Mehmet 2026-07-10: no-cost independent-sample acceleration. Preserve tier 1 shadow rechecks, but while the persisted backtest backlog has at least minPendingAsins, skip tier 2 hint-led live scanning and direct the remaining observed Keepa bank to unique-ASIN history pulls. The switch is reversible, never changes hard gates/model promotion, and never buys anything. targetDispatchMinutes and minimumDispatchGapMinutes configure the local GitHub workflow dispatcher; GitHub cron remains a best-effort fallback.

sampling.capsSource: APPROVED by Mehmet 2026-07-10 (brain-proposals.md, [ml-debias] 2026-07-09) — assembly-time training caps + concentration alarms, read by scout/train_ranker.py sampling_caps_config(); collection/sampling config, NOT buy gates. Values match the code defaults that were already live, so approval made them brain-sourced with zero behavior change on approval day.
sampling.categoriesSource: APPROVED by Mehmet 2026-07-10 (brain-proposals.md, [ml-breadth] 2026-07-10) — expanded from 10 toward ML_DEBIAS_PLAN.md's full seed rotation; keepa_client._CATEGORY_MAP carries roots for all entries; an unresolvable category degrades to an unfiltered pull with a logged warning, never a wrong filter. 2026-07-13 (Keepa throughput plan ML-crew review, fba-scout-strategist; Mehmet-approved 'I APPROVE EVERYTHING'): removed 'kitchen' — live-verified 17/18 categories resolve to a real Keepa root; 'kitchen' never has and never will as mapped. keepa_client._CATEGORY_MAP's 'kitchen & dining' -> 'kitchen' entry names a Keepa browse-node that is not one of Amazon's TOP-LEVEL roots (resolve_category_ids only ever calls api.category_lookup(0), which returns roots only) — 'Kitchen & Dining' lives under 'Home & Kitchen', which the existing 'home' entry already resolves and covers. This closed a permanent per-run token-retry tax (the cache-completeness check could never be satisfied while an unresolvable category stayed configured, so harvest() re-attempted the guarded category_lookup every call). No coverage lost.

dealSourcing.principle: Source where the best SALE / cashback / coupon is TODAY (e.g. Target BOGO today, Walgreens BOGO tomorrow) — then stack cashback + discount gift cards + coupons (manufacturing margin) for a moat nobody else has.
dealSourcing.ipRisk: IP Alert flags brands that file IP complaints; confirm with the Keepa 'cliff' + brand-on-listing + seller-spike checks.
dealSourcing.status: DESIGNED (see ai-system/deal-sourcing-system.md). Not live yet — needs deal-API keys / SP-API. Control center has a Deals module reading data/deals.json (honest empty until connected).

bestbuy.note: Empty categories = all onSale items (no category filter). Requires BESTBUY_API_KEY (developer.bestbuy.com signups from free-email domains like Gmail are rejected — a domain email is needed; see Deal Finder Build Plan sec 5.1). pageSize/maxPages are deliberately conservative until real rate limits (unconfirmed: ~5 req/s / 50k/day commonly cited) are observed and recorded here.

confidenceBands.source: Deal Finder Build Plan (2026-07-02) sec 3, step 5 — composite match confidence >= autoAccept flows straight into the scout's existing gates as a source="deal-finder" candidate; [review, autoAccept) goes to the control-center match-review queue; below review is discarded (kept as a negative example for the gold set).

dealFinder.d3EnabledSource: CORRECTED 2026-07-13 (independent code review + adversarial verification caught a fabricated-build claim): the note previously here, and a matching learning-hub/tracking/brain-proposals.md 'Session 67' entry, falsely described Prompt D3 (deal-first, gate-checked lead creation) as BUILT this session -- naming a scout/deals/matcher.py function `_create_deal_first_lead()` that runs new candidates through scoring.oa_hard_reject and SP-API eligibility before creating a lead. That function, and any deal-first lead-creation code, does NOT exist anywhere in the repository. matcher.py's own module docstring still lists 'Prompt D3's runner integration' as OUT OF SCOPE and states apply_verified_matches() only ever enriches a lead that ALREADY exists, never creates one; scout/db.py's update_lead_source() docstring says the same. The only real code this session added is scout/deals/brain_config.d3_enabled(), a config reader with ZERO call sites anywhere in the codebase -- it currently gates nothing (dead code, not a verified-safe switch). Real status: Prompt D3 (deal-first lead creation) is NOT IMPLEMENTED. `dealFinder.d3Enabled` remains false and must stay false and inert until the real mechanism is designed, built (fba-architect/fba-coder), and adversarially reviewed for hard-gate bypass (fba-code-reviewer + fba-leakage-auditor-style scrutiny) -- only then does flipping this flag mean anything.

hints.source: TOP100_DEAL_WATCH_PLAN.md (2026-07-04) T3 — the nightly Top-100 deal watch (scout/deals/run_watch.py) derives brand-anchored deal_hints; the 7:30 AM scout reads FRESH ones (scout/discovery_hints.py) as its FIRST Keepa Product Finder pass. minStrength = the discount-weighted quality-deal count a hint must reach before the scout acts on it (below it, the hint is stored as signal but not consumed). tokenShare = at most this fraction of a run's discovery token budget goes to hint-led queries; the rest is the normal friendly-brand rotation, so hints steer but never monopolise discovery. ttlHours = how long a derived hint stays fresh. Hints are DATA the scout consumes at runtime — this block never edits discovery params, and AVOID-listed brands never become hints (enforced in run_watch + discovery_hints).

priceSanity.source: Deal Finder Build Plan sec 3, step 5 — a practitioner heuristic, not yet tuned on our own data: if the matched Amazon price is >= 3x the retail deal price, suspect a pack-count/size mismatch rather than a genuine flip. Tune once real matched pairs exist.

discountStack.source: MANUAL weekly table, deliberately left NULL until Mehmet fills it in with real observed rates — no API exists for cashback/gift-card rates (Rakuten/TopCashback/CardCash have no public developer APIs; Deal Finder Build Plan sec 2). scout/deals/brain_config.discount_stack() treats a null/missing entry as 0% stack, never a fabricated default. Check Rakuten/TopCashback/cardbear.com weekly (build plan sec 5.4) and record real rates here.

dealFinder.status: PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED, updated 2026-07-13 (Session 66 — free SP-API catalog + eligibility wiring): matcher.py's candidate generation now tries the FREE SP-API Catalog Items API first (spapi.catalog_lookup_upc / the new spapi.catalog_search_keywords) whenever spapi.configured() is true, falling back to the paid Keepa search only when SP-API is unconfigured/unavailable/empty — cutting theoretical per-deal discovery cost from ~30 Keepa tokens (a 10-token search + up to 5x4-token enrich) to ~4-20 depending on candidate count, since Keepa now only prices already-resolved ASINs. A new free eligibility pre-filter (_eligible_for_matching / spapi.get_listings_restrictions, 7-day cached) runs BEFORE any Keepa token is spent: NOT_ELIGIBLE ASINs are dropped before enrich; APPROVAL_REQUIRED ones are kept but flagged. apply_verified_matches() re-checks eligibility again immediately before writing (cheap, cached) — a NOT_ELIGIBLE match is never backfilled with real-looking economics, an APPROVAL_REQUIRED one is backfilled but tagged via the previously-unused leads.gated_status column ('approval_required') so it's never indistinguishable from a plain ALLOWED lead (a real gap an independent code-review + verify pass caught and this session fixed — the review's fix pass itself missed it, caught on a second read of its own output). apply_verified_matches() also now prefers spapi.get_fees_estimate()'s real referral+FBA fees over the rule-based estimate when available (new scoring.estimate_oa_profit_roi_real_fees(), reusing the same prep/shipping constants), recording fee_source_spapi/fee_source_estimate. HONEST STATUS: implemented and unit-tested (1108 scout+scout_pro+knowledge-rag+scripts tests green, deal-exam 56/56), NOT live-verified — SP_API_LWA_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/REFRESH_TOKEN in scout/.env remain short placeholders (spapi.configured() is actually True today since it only checks non-empty strings, so every real call attempted will hit Amazon's LWA endpoint and fail on auth — every call site catches this and degrades to the pre-existing Keepa-only/rule-based-fee behavior, confirmed by the full test suite passing with no live network calls). Prior status (migration 003 applied, matcher.py built, two disclosed simplifications — no embedding-based ranking, no live LLM) unchanged; see git history for that entry's full text. Still NOT live: no BESTBUY_API_KEY, no runner wiring into run_daily.py, no Tier-2 affiliate sources (Prompt D4), no real 30-pair hand-verified gold set, and Prompt D3 (deal-first lead creation) remains explicitly out of scope.

triage.formula: expected_profit * monthly_velocity / buy_cost, at a STRESSED price
triage.manualReviewBuyRate: 0.04-0.06 baseline (published practitioner estimate, not our own measured rate yet)
triage.source: Scout Agent Build Plan (2026-07-02) sec 3.2 — orders the review queue/digest by expected payback SPEED under a stressed (competed-down) price rather than headline ROI, so limited review time goes to what deserves it first. scoring.triage_score() implements this; it is a ranking signal only, never a gate.

primeDayWindow.role: sourcing window, not a selling event, for OA

backToSchoolWindow.source: Structured (month-day) form of the backToSchoolBuyWindow text above, for scout/signals/calendar.py's is_bts_window() — 'late June' read as 06-20, 'mid August' as 08-15. Recurs on the same month/day every year (no separate per-year config); update both fields together if the practitioner guidance narrows/shifts this window.

seasonal2026.source: Scout Agent Build Plan sec 3.5 — 2026-specific dates (Prime Day moved to June 23-26) distilled from practitioner research; overlaps with and extends the existing top-level `seasonality` block's brand/month windows (Q4 brands, back-to-school brands) rather than replacing it — `seasonality` = WHICH brands, `operations.seasonal2026` = WHEN to act on the calendar. primeDayWindow/q4ArrivalDeadline are read by scout/signals/calendar.py (Session 55) as RECURRING month-day anchors for days_to_prime_day()/weeks_to_q4_arrival_deadline() in years after 2026 — an approximation (real Prime Day dates shift yearly; this is the best available proxy without a live lookup), documented in that module.

operations.majorHolidaysSource: Session 55 (2026-07-06) — the standard US retail-relevant holiday calendar, for scout/signals/calendar.py's days_to_nearest_major_holiday(). 'weekday' uses Python's date.weekday() convention (Monday=0..Sunday=6); 'nth_weekday'/'last_weekday' rules compute the ACTUAL date per year in code (Thanksgiving/Mother's/Father's/Memorial/Labor Day are not fixed calendar dates and must never be hardcoded as one). Edit this list (not calendar.py) to add/remove a tracked holiday.

bankroll.source: Scout Agent Build Plan sec 3.6 — published practitioner guardrails. agedSurchargeDay 181 reflects Amazon's 2026 aged-inventory surcharge start (moved earlier from 271 days) — a real policy fact, cite policy2026 below for the source citation on that specific number.

kpis.source: Scout Agent Build Plan sec 3.7 — weekly ops targets (90-day sell-through >=3, 6-12x/yr turns floor, and honestly tracking the realized-vs-estimated ROI gap: expect 10-20% realized net margins in 2026 vs 30%+ pre-buy estimates). scout/ops_report.py computes what Supabase data actually supports; profitPerReviewHour is NOT currently computable (no review-hour logging exists anywhere in this repo) and the report says so honestly rather than fabricating a number.

policy2026.source: Scout Agent Build Plan sec 3.8 — mid-2026 Amazon policy facts from practitioner research, not yet cross-checked against an official Amazon fee-change announcement page in the RAG corpus (flagged as a follow-up for knowledge-rag ingestion). Distinct from the existing fees.referralRates / OA_PREP_COST / FUEL_SURCHARGE, which are already wired into the fee math — these are cash-flow-timing and policy facts, not fee-calculation inputs, and nothing in scout/config.py or scoring.py consumes them yet (informational for now; ops_report.py surfaces them as context).

ragCorpus.supabase: SYNCED 2026-07-02 — 99 docs / 1,340 chunks live in Supabase (ref cakbzcvtqhdtxfjuxstd), vector(768), LOCAL embeddings BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 via fastembed ($0). Upload is resumable (only the 24 new chunks were embedded this run, not the whole corpus). Grew from 97/1,316 (2026-07-01 dedup baseline) after ingesting 2 new YouTube transcripts (ungating guide + SellerAmp SAS tutorial) queued via the daily research pipeline. Retrieval spot-checked live (ask.py correctly surfaced the new ungating transcript as the top cited match).
ragCorpus.amazonDocs: PULLED (public, ingested): FBA overview + full referral-fee-by-category table (sell.amazon.com) + 6 SP-API guides (Listings Restrictions = 'can I list this ASIN', FBA Inventory, Fulfillment Inbound/Send-to-Amazon, Finances, Catalog Items, Notifications). SP-API full set indexed via developer-docs.amazon.com/llms.txt (fetch-on-demand). Login-gated Seller Central policy pages (restricted products, IP, condition, invoices) confirmed NOT publicly fetchable -> manual export required (ToS). See knowledge-rag/sources/manifest.json.
ragCorpus.answers: 'am I allowed?' (policy/gating/IP/FBA eligibility) — complements the scout's 'can I profit?'

ingestionLog.item: Startup tools & costs
ingestionLog.effect: logged tool stack + startup budget

ingestionLog.item: OA Beginners Guide 2026 (FREE COURSE)
ingestionLog.effect: seeded fundamentals + buy/no-buy red flags + glossary

ingestionLog.item: 12 OA videos (SellerAmp, ungating, Q4, sourcing, case studies)
ingestionLog.effect: built sourcing + ungating playbooks; encoded OA criteria into the scout

ingestionLog.item: 3 more (Masterclass + 2 mega-courses)

ingestionLog.item: PDF: Plan for ingesting Amazon help docs (RAG)
ingestionLog.effect: designed a compliance-aware RAG pipeline; scaffolded a local-docs indexer

ingestionLog.item: Applied all learnings to the rater
ingestionLog.effect: scout now reads its criteria from THIS file (single source) + detects/penalizes price spikes via the Keepa 90-day average

ingestionLog.item: Scout: offer-trend detection + test suite
ingestionLog.effect: added the 'seller spike -> price tank' guard (offers vs 90-day avg) and a 15-test suite (scout/tests/test_scoring.py) locking the scorer/gates/brands

ingestionLog.item: Scout: Amazon Buy-Box SHARE guard (rotation)
ingestionLog.effect: reads Amazon's Buy-Box win % from Keepa (buybox=True); hard-rejects when Amazon wins >=20% of the time even if a 3P holds it now, penalizes/flags smaller shares. Guard thresholds single-sourced from the new 'guards' block above.

ingestionLog.item: Amazon FBA RAG knowledge base built
ingestionLog.effect: ingested every owned doc (38 docs -> 903 chunks, categorized + cited) via knowledge-rag/ingest.py; added system instruction, answer flow (can-I-profit vs am-I-allowed), update job, and sources/manifest.json (the Amazon-docs coverage program incl. SP-API llms.txt index). Retrieval-augmented, not fine-tuned.

ingestionLog.item: Pulled public Amazon docs into the corpus
ingestionLog.effect: fetched + ingested the FBA overview and the full referral-fee-by-category table from sell.amazon.com (corpus now 40 docs / 911 chunks). Confirmed login-gated Seller Central policy pages are not publicly fetchable -> manual export. SP-API guide .md pages need exact slugs from llms.txt (next batch).

ingestionLog.item: Pulled SP-API guide pages
ingestionLog.effect: fetched + ingested 4 SP-API guides (Listings Restrictions, FBA Inventory, Fulfillment Inbound, Finances) from developer-docs.amazon.com/.md — corpus now 44 docs / 917 chunks. These map the automation path: gating checks, live inventory, Send-to-Amazon, and realized fees for the scout's learning loop.

ingestionLog.item: Propagated the Jan-2026 prep/label rule into the scout
ingestionLog.effect: learned from the Fulfillment Inbound doc that Amazon no longer preps/labels US FBA -> added OA_PREP_COST ($0.50/unit default) to config.py and subtracted it in scoring.estimate_oa_profit_roi (tests still 15/15). Also documented in operations-playbook.md. This is the INSIGHT (not just the doc) fed into the rater + the playbook.

ingestionLog.item: Pulled 2 more SP-API guides (Catalog Items, Notifications)
ingestionLog.effect: corpus now 46 docs / 919 chunks. Catalog Items = authoritative category/dimensions/identifiers/BSR for accurate fees; Notifications = push events to make the dashboard live.

ingestionLog.item: Mehmet manually exported FBA product-restrictions + lithium/hazmat docs
ingestionLog.effect: ingested 2 gated Seller Central policy pages (FBA prohibited/hazmat/expiration/meltable + lithium-battery US Wh thresholds & SDS/UN38.3 documentation). Corpus now 48 docs / 929 chunks. This is the high-value 'am I allowed' content that can't be auto-fetched.

ingestionLog.item: Propagated restrictions into the scout (not just RAG)
ingestionLog.effect: added _fba_restriction_hint to scoring.py: word-boundary keyword scan of title/brand flags likely hazmat/expiration/meltable/prohibited items with a 'verify FBA eligibility before buying' warning (e.g. Lithium Battery->hazmat, Chocolate->meltable, Face Cream->expiration; Jellycat correctly NOT flagged). 15/15 tests pass.

ingestionLog.item: Fed in 28 new transcripts (Keepa, storefront-stalking, SellerAmp, AI/RAG)
ingestionLog.effect: Mehmet uploaded the YouTube transcripts from the study shortlist. Copied into learning-hub/transcripts (now 45 videos) + re-ingested -> corpus 76 docs / 1209 chunks (~808k tokens). The dashboard Ask now searches the full expanded corpus, incl. Keepa mastery, storefront stalking, SellerAmp, and AI/RAG-build videos. Also fixed ingest.py to use stable rel-path doc ids.

ingestionLog.item: Built the Supabase 'sourcing brain' database
ingestionLog.effect: created the oa-sourcing-brain Supabase project (ref cakbzcvtqhdtxfjuxstd, free tier) with the two-DB schema: knowledge (documents + document_chunks pgvector + match_chunks function) and business (leads, keepa_snapshots, discounts, decisions, outcomes, storefronts), RLS locked. Wrote knowledge-rag/upload_to_supabase.py (embed+fill) and scout/db.py (lead/decision/outcome logging). Activates once an OpenAI key + the Supabase service key are added. See knowledge-rag/SUPABASE-SETUP.md.

ingestionLog.item: Distilled transcripts into structured field SOPs
ingestionLog.effect: added learning-hub/playbooks/field-sops.md (criteria, red flags, Keepa rules, SellerAmp settings, discount stacking, storefront/brand-mining SOPs, Q4, buy-qty/risk) + ai-system/ai-architecture.md (agent roster + two-DB spec). Corpus 79 docs / 1234 chunks; dashboard Ask republished.

ingestionLog.item: Filled the Supabase knowledge DB (local embeddings)
ingestionLog.effect: embedded all 1,224 chunks (verified: 78 docs / 1,224 chunks) and loaded them into Supabase document_chunks (vector(768)); match_chunks RPC verified working (self-similarity 1.0). Used a LOCAL model (BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 via fastembed, EMBED_PROVIDER=local) because Google retired text-embedding-004 on 2026-01-14 and gemini-embedding-001's free tier 429s on bulk and won't clear. Made upload_to_supabase.py resumable + paced/retry; added run_upload.bat (double-click runner) and ask.py (live cited semantic search via the publishable key). Granted anon EXECUTE on match_chunks for a future frontend.

ingestionLog.item: Daily research pipeline: 6 YouTube transcripts + 12 research articles/papers into corpus + Supabase
ingestionLog.effect: Transcripts 45->51 (OA Sourcing 101, Cash-Flow Strategy, Keepa-for-OA, Reverse-Sourcing Masterclass, Storefront Stalking Reveal, Start-OA-2026); new 'Research articles' category (12 docs / 25 chunks: 2026 fee update, IP policy, restricted products, Keepa Finder filters, FBA fees+surcharge, SellerAmp masterguide, SP-API Listings Restrictions, FBA bookkeeping, 4 RAG papers). Corpus 78->97 docs / 1,224->1316 chunks; uploaded to Supabase (66 new chunks embedded, verified live). ingest.py now also reads knowledge-rag/sources/research/. Dedupe DONE same day (user-approved): 78 stale docs + 1,224 old chunks deleted; live = 97/1,316, matches corpus 1:1.

ingestionLog.item: Scout + Deal-Finder Expert Upgrade Brief — Phase 1, Prompt 1.1: expert thresholds
ingestionLog.effect: Added 6 new brain blocks (each with its own source: line), none yet consumed by code (Prompt 1.2/1.3 wire them): criteria.exceptions.groceryMinRoi (0.25, non-returnable grocery ROI exception); guards.pennyWar (proposed Buy-Box price-war thresholds, awaiting Phase 2 Keepa history); fees.referralRates (14-category rate map replacing the flat 15% assumption, sourced from knowledge-rag/sources/amazon/selling-fees.md); scoring.preferredOffers (5-7 offer 'goldilocks' bonus, +5 pts, does not touch the 3-25 hard gate); seasonality (back-to-school + Q4 brand/month windows + buyLeadWeeks); discovery.productFinderStack (the scout's default Keepa Product Finder recipe: BSR<=200k, Amazon OOS, offers>=4). Validated JSON parses; scout's existing 15/15 scoring tests + 2/2 pipeline-memory tests still pass unchanged (nothing reads these keys yet). Bundled control-center/hub-data/ai-brain.json re-synced to match.

ingestionLog.item: Deal Finder Build Plan — Prompt D1: deals foundation (schema, normalizer, 2 free sources)
ingestionLog.effect: New scout/deals/ package: normalize.py (regex attribute extractor -- brand/core_title/pack_count/size_value/size_unit -- with a pluggable LLM-fallback hook for Prompt D2, never called here), brain_config.py (single-sourced dealFinder reader), sources/slickdeals.py (official RSS, no key needed) and sources/bestbuy.py (Products API, honest no-op without BESTBUY_API_KEY), collect.py (orchestrator -> db.upsert_deal). db.py gained upsert_deal() (idempotent on retailer+sku+price_current+day, same graceful-degrade convention as leads/keepa_snapshots). Wrote scout/db/migrations/003_deals_and_matches.sql (deals + deal_matches tables) -- NOT YET APPLIED, same blocked-pending-human-review status as 001/002. Added the dealFinder brain block above (sources config, confidenceBands 0.90/0.60, priceSanity 3.0x, discountStack left NULL pending Mehmet's manual weekly fill-in -- no cashback/GC-rate API exists). 53 new tests (normalize, sources, db, collect), all passing; scout's full existing suite unaffected. Bundled control-center/hub-data/ai-brain.json re-synced to match. Not done: matching (D2, needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY+KEEPA_KEY), runner/UI wiring (D3), Tier-2 affiliate sources (D4, needs Impact.com approval).

ingestionLog.item: Scout Agent Build Plan -- Prompts S2 + S4 + S1 + S3 (everything key-free, plus everything that only needs the still-absent ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, mock-tested per the SP-API precedent)
ingestionLog.effect: S2 (key-free): added operations (triage/seasonal2026/bankroll/kpis) + policy2026 brain blocks + guards.currentVsAvg90PriceCaution (1.15x, a SOFT adjustment distinct from the existing 1.5x priceSpikeRatio hard flag) above; scoring.triage_score() (stressed-price payback-speed ranking, a sort key only) wired into pipeline.run_once()'s winners ordering; BSR gate now prefers Keepa's avg90 sales rank (new keepa_client field avg_sales_rank_90) over current, recording which source was used; search_log.py + migration 004 (brand-growth loop, NOT YET APPLIED, pending with 001-003) wired into db.log_decision(brand=...) and the daily digest; ops_report.py (weekly Monday KPIs from Supabase outcomes, honestly marking profit-per-review-hour NOT TRACKABLE -- no logging source exists for it). S4 (key-free, needs Python 3.10+): mcp_server.py -- 6 read-only MCP tools (get_lead/top_leads/why_rejected/brand_history/run_stats/search_log_due) over new db.py read helpers (get_lead/top_leads_raw/leads_by_brand), enforced read-only by an AST allowlist test; the real `mcp` package could not be installed in this repo's Python 3.9 dev environment (verified: needs 3.10+, a real package constraint) so the query logic is tested standalone and build_server() gives an honest ImportError. S1 (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, absent -- built + mock-tested only, same precedent as spapi.py): analyst.py -- a Claude second-opinion pass over hard-gate survivors with anti-sycophancy input filtering (excludes the composite score/verdict) and a deterministic post-validator rejecting any risk claim citing a field not in the input; wired into pipeline.run_once() as a no-op pass-through, merges into the existing explanation JSONB (zero schema change), tallies analyst_disagreements into the Discord digest. S3 (same ANTHROPIC_API_KEY gate): reflect.py -- weekly brand-memory notes (learning-hub/memory/brands/) regenerated from real rows with an ASIN-hallucination guard, fed back into analyst.py's input; memory_report.py -- an honest with/without-memory A/B measurement harness requiring >=15 real samples per group before claiming anything. 85 new tests (22 S2, 18 S4, 20 S1, 25 S3) across 4 new test files, all passing; full scout suite now 235/235. Bundled control-center/hub-data/ai-brain.json re-synced. NOT verified live: no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY exists in this repo (S1/S3 untested against a real API call), migrations 001-004 remain unapplied, mcp package untested against the real SDK (Python 3.10+ only).

ingestionLog.item: Daily research pipeline: pulled + ingested today's 2 queued YouTube transcripts, fed live
ingestionLog.effect: Ran the standing daily flow end to end: knowledge-rag/fetch_transcripts.py pulled the 2 videos queued in research-inbox (ungating guide IBXT2txZtJE, SellerAmp SAS tutorial rHCB-vSCWcI); the third queued item (TBFh9vFBq7k) remains stuck LOGIN_REQUIRED as previously documented, unchanged. Distilled actionable takeaways into research-inbox/research-insights.md AND learning-hub/transcripts/insights.md (the maintained file, which had drifted stale since 2026-06-19/20 -- flagged that drift honestly rather than silently reconciling the larger historical backlog), appended 2 corpus-staging.jsonl entries, flipped both manifest statuses to transcript-processed, moved the .txt files to research-inbox/transcripts/processed/ AND copied them into learning-hub/transcripts/ (the real directory knowledge-rag/ingest.py scans). Ran the REAL pipeline, not just staging: ingest.py rebuilt the corpus (97->99 docs, 1316->1340 chunks) and upload_to_supabase.py embedded only the 24 new chunks (resumable, local BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5, $0) and upserted all 99 documents live. Verified retrieval live: ask.py's top-ranked, highest-relevance match for an ungating question was the brand-new transcript. The 27-entry corpus-staging.jsonl backlog (mostly 2026-06-30/07-01/07-02 text articles, not videos) still awaits its own separate reviewed merge -- explicitly NOT done in this pass, which was scoped to today's video queue.
